context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29 after continuing generalized streaming lane. User emphasized: anything not LLM-specific should be generalized and reusable. We now have crate-level generic SSE primitives plus LLM-specific bridge semantics layered on top. WASM remains useful for plugin isolation, but native/target-neutral Tractor owns reusable stream framing and host-proxied provider transport.
current_tasks: Latest commits after prior handoff: 803a97d3 feat(tractor): add generic sse reader primitive; 53638588 feat(tractor): add incremental llm stream reader seam; 49231f06 feat(tractor): read stream bridge through sse seam; e87e0218 docs(agent): note stream bridge reader seam.

Generic SSE primitive now includes SseDataEventBuffer plus read_sse_data_events_limited(reader,max_len,on_event) in packages/tractor/src/streaming/sse_events.rs. It is target-neutral, provider-neutral, enforces body limit, propagates callback errors, returns full body, and emits complete data frames incrementally.

LLM-specific stream code in packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge/llm_stream_events.rs now has store_stream_agent_response_chunks_from_reader. It uses the generic SSE reader, parses provider JSON payloads, stores AgentResponse partials as events arrive, and returns final_body,last_sequence,stored_chunks.

complete-http-stream in packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge/core.rs now sends the host-enforced HTTP request once and reads successful response bytes through store_stream_agent_response_chunks_from_reader(resp.into_reader(), MAX_LLM_RESPONSE_BODY_LEN). This is closer to live streaming, but final response semantics are still unresolved so pi-agent readiness remains false.
llm_complete_http was refactored to reuse send_llm_http_post, preserving route/body/header/auth enforcement and existing buffered path behavior.

ROADMAP updated: Tractor complete-http-stream reads through generic SSE seam and persists complete SSE frames as they arrive; remaining TODO is finalizing live provider stream response semantics before enabling streaming_reader_available.
blockers: streaming_reader_available() remains false. Provider stream:true still disabled from pi-agent because final response handling for SSE provider streams is not solved end-to-end.

complete-http-stream can read/persist SSE frames as the response body is read, but no integration/harness test yet proves partial AgentResponse nodes are visible to WebSocket clients during a still-open provider response.

Final response semantics for true provider SSE are unresolved: existing pi-agent provider parser expects JSON final_body; SSE final_body is not JSON. Need synthesize JSON, change WIT result, or adapt pi-agent final assembly before enabling.
Current local stack is large and unpushed; GitHub Actions not run for it yet.
Disk after cleanup: ~12GB free; Rust targets ~4.86GB.
next_actions: Add an integration-style test around complete-http-stream with a local fake HTTP SSE server. Ideally keep it unit-scoped in Tractor without full pi-agent harness first. Verify route enforcement still applies and partial AgentResponse rows are stored from streamed SSE bytes.

Design final SSE response semantics. Options: host synthesizes provider-compatible final JSON from accumulated deltas; WIT stream-response-result carries a stream summary/content; pi-agent final response is assembled from partial chunks; or dual path for buffered JSON vs provider SSE.
Only after final semantics + harness proof should streaming_reader_available() flip true and provider request bodies emit stream:true.
Consider extracting OpenAI/Anthropic stream delta parsing to a shared target-neutral crate/module later to avoid divergence with pi-agent provider_runtime/stream_events.rs.
If pushing current stack, use git push then gh run watch --exit-status; do not manually poll CI.
open_questions: What final_body should complete-http-stream return for true SSE provider streams so existing pi-agent parsers do not fail?
Should host directly store AgentResponse partials long-term, or should it store generic StreamChunk observations and project AgentResponse for clients?
Should read_sse_data_events_limited be promoted beyond crate-private if other packages need it later?
How should partial stream errors/cancellation be represented after some chunks are already stored?
Should generic streaming primitives support non-SSE chunk observers too, or wait until a concrete non-SSE use case appears?
key_decisions_pending: AgentResponse direct chunks vs generic StreamChunk/dual-write.
Shared parser extraction vs host-owned parser duplication.
When to push the large local stack.
